# Phase 26 — Documentation Site (DocFX → GitHub Pages)

**Status:** Planned (written 2026-06-02). A public documentation website at
`https://shadowdusk.github.io/ShadowDusk/`, generated from the solution: an
**auto-generated managed API reference** (from XML doc-comments), **how-to / examples
/ samples** pages, and **conceptual/explanation** pages — built with **DocFX** and
published by GitHub Actions to GitHub Pages. Inspired by (not a clone of) the DocFX
site at `https://kaltinril.github.io/Kernsmith/`.

**Depends on:**
- **Phase 30** (cross-platform CI) — the docs build is a GitHub Actions workflow; it can ship independently but shares CI conventions (and Pages is CI infrastructure). Keep the docs workflow self-contained so it does not block on the full RID matrix.
- The **doc-accuracy sweep** already landed (2026-06-02): `README.md`, `docs/references/compilation-pipeline.md`, `docs/glsl-uniform-naming.md`, `docs/research.md`, `monogame_runtime_mgfx_compiler_research.md`, and the Slang `RESTORE.md` were corrected to current reality. The site reuses these as source-of-truth, so it must not re-introduce the stale claims they fixed (see *Content-accuracy guards*).

**Blocks:** nothing in the product pipeline. This is a discoverability/onboarding deliverable for users and contributors — the public face of "add the package, call the API."

> The product is the in-memory `IShaderCompiler` library (see `CLAUDE.md` → THE PURPOSE). This site documents that product (and its delivery shapes + samples); it is **not** itself a product surface. Do not let doc-site work redefine the goal.

---

## Overview

Three content types, mirroring the structure the user pointed at (a Diátaxis-style split):

1. **The code** — an **API reference** auto-generated by DocFX from the managed projects' XML doc-comments (namespaces → types → members → parameters). This is the bulk of the *recurring* value and the bulk of the *one-time* work (most public API currently lacks `///` comments — see *The hard prerequisite*).
2. **Examples / samples / how-to** — getting-started, the in-memory quickstart, the drop-in-`mgfxc` and MGCB guides, the in-browser KNI/Blazor walkthrough (reusing `docs/HOWTO-WASM-KNI.md`), and per-sample pages for `ShaderFiddle.Web` / `ShaderViewer` / `mgcb`.
3. **Explanations** — architecture pages: the faithful pipeline, FX9 pre-parser + preprocessor, reflection (DXIL oracle vs managed `SpirvReflector`), the GLSL dialect rewrite, the `.mgfx`/MojoShader format, the DirectX `vkd3d` path, and the WASM frontend.

DocFX is the right tool: it is the .NET-standard static-site generator, produces the API reference from the same XML doc-comments developers write in-IDE, renders conceptual Markdown alongside, ships a search-enabled "modern" template, and is exactly what the reference site (KernSmith) uses.

---

## The hard prerequisite — XML doc-comments (the real cost)

A DocFX readiness audit (2026-06-02) found public-API doc-comment coverage at **~35–40%**, very unevenly:

| Project | Impl status | Public types (est.) | With `<summary>` | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| **ShadowDusk.Core** | implemented | ~56 | ~12 (~20%) | **Biggest gap & highest visibility.** The consumer contract is undocumented: `IShaderCompiler`, `CompiledShader`, `CompilerOptions`, `Result<T,TError>`/`Unit`, `ShaderError`, `PlatformTarget`, and the whole `Reflection/*` + `Preprocessor/*` families. **Document first.** |
| ShadowDusk.HLSL | implemented | ~34 | ~26 | Best-documented internals (Ast/Lexer/Dxc/Reflection/Vkd3d); gaps are member-level on the compiler classes. Mostly contributor-facing. |
| ShadowDusk.GLSL | implemented | ~5 | ~4 | Small & good; only the concrete `SpirvCrossGlslTranspiler` and `ISpirvToGlslTranspiler.Transpile` need a summary. |
| **ShadowDusk.Compiler** | implemented | 1 | 0 | `EffectCompiler : IShaderCompiler` — **the product entry point** — has rich inline `//` comments but **zero** `<summary>`. High value-per-line. |
| ShadowDusk.Wasm | implemented | 1 | 1 | `WasmShaderCompiler` has an excellent class summary (method lacks one). Documented **conceptually**, excluded from API metadata (see below). |
| ShadowDusk.Cli | implemented | 0 public | — | All internal; `mgfxc` is a CLI contract → CLI reference page, not API docs. |
| ShadowDusk.Metal | **stub** | 1 (empty) | 0 | `MslEmitter` is `public sealed class MslEmitter { }`. **Exclude** from API ref; mark "future". |
| ShadowDusk.MgcbPlugin | **stub** | 0 (no source) | — | Only a `.csproj`. **Exclude**; document MGCB Tier-1 conceptually. |

So the dominant task is **writing `<summary>`/`<param>`/`<returns>` comments**, starting with `ShadowDusk.Core`'s consumer contract and `EffectCompiler`. Caveat: the repo sets `TreatWarningsAsErrors=true` and a high `WarningLevel`, so enabling `GenerateDocumentationFile` repo-wide turns **CS1591 (missing XML comment)** into **build errors**. Strategy: enable XML docs per-project as its comments are filled in, and/or `<NoWarn>$(NoWarn);CS1591</NoWarn>` during the transition — never block the normal build on incomplete doc-comments.

---

## Scope & Non-Goals

**In scope:**
- A `docfx.json` at repo root; an `index.md` landing page; a top-level `toc.yml`; conceptual Markdown under a docs content tree; an `api/index.md` API landing page.
- DocFX **`metadata`** generating the API reference from the **four library projects** (`Core`, `HLSL`, `GLSL`, `Compiler`).
- XML doc-comments for the public API, **Core's consumer contract first**, then `EffectCompiler`, then the documented interfaces' methods.
- A GitHub Actions workflow that builds the site and deploys to GitHub Pages.
- Reuse of the existing accurate docs (move/include, single source of truth).
- The new conceptual/how-to pages listed below.

**Out of scope / Non-Goals:**
- Documenting the **stubs** (`Metal`, `MgcbPlugin`) as if implemented — they are excluded/flagged "future".
- Adding API metadata for **`ShadowDusk.Wasm`** (net8.0-browser + Razor SDK + a `VerifyDxcWasmPresent` build gate make Roslyn metadata extraction fragile in CI) — it is documented **conceptually** via `docs/HOWTO-WASM-KNI.md`.
- A custom theme/branding pass, versioned (multi-version) docs, localization, or a custom domain — all follow-ons.
- Surfacing raw research (`docs/research.md`, `monogame_runtime_mgfx_compiler_research.md`) or `plan/`/`CLAUDE.md` as site pages — they stay in-repo (distilled into Contributing where useful).

---

## Architecture & key decisions

- **Tool:** DocFX 2.x (the maintained `dotnet/docfx`, NuGet `docfx`), installed as a pinned global tool (`dotnet tool install --global docfx --version <pin>`) — **not** the legacy `docfx.console` MSBuild package or DocFX v3. Template: `default` + `modern` (search enabled).
- **API metadata scope:** the four plain-`net8.0` library projects only. Excluded: `Wasm` (browser TFM/Razor/build-gate), `Cli` (no public API), `Metal` + `MgcbPlugin` (stubs). Rationale and the full list live in the workflow design notes.
- **Project-Pages subpath:** the site serves under `…github.io/ShadowDusk/` (a subpath). DocFX's modern template emits root-relative links; inject `<base href="/ShadowDusk/">` into `_site/*.html` post-build (or use the template's base-url option) so CSS/JS/search/inter-page links resolve.
- **Generated output is not committed:** add `api/` (generated `*.yml`) and `_site/` to `.gitignore`; DocFX regenerates them each run. A hand-written `api/index.md` (not overwritten by metadata) is the API landing page.
- **One source of truth:** reuse the high-quality existing docs by **moving them into the content tree and updating in-repo cross-links**, or via DocFX `[!INCLUDE]`, so the in-repo `docs/` and the site never diverge. `CLAUDE.md` warns drift has bitten before — do not duplicate.
- **README ↔ landing sync:** keep `README.md` the repo front-door; the site `index.md` includes shared README fragments (what-it-does, backend matrix, principles) rather than re-typing them, so a README edit updates the site.

### Proposed information architecture (`toc.yml`)

```
Home
Getting Started      → Overview · Installation · In-Memory Quickstart (IShaderCompiler) · Restore Native Tools
Guides               → Drop-in mgfxc · MGCB Content Pipeline (Tier-1) · In-Browser (KNI/Blazor WASM) · Parameters & Caveats
Architecture         → The Faithful Pipeline · FX9 Pre-Parser & Preprocessor · Reflection · GLSL Dialect Rewrite ·
                       MGFX/MojoShader Format · DirectX DXBC (vkd3d) Path · WASM In-Browser Frontend
Alternative Backends → Metal (future) · Vulkan (future) · DirectX-in-WASM (Phase 4.1 spike)
Samples              → ShaderFiddle.Web · ShaderViewer · MGCB Sample
CLI Reference        → mgfxc usage (positional <SourceFile> <OutputFile>, /Profile, /Debug, /I, --mgfx-version)
API Reference        → DocFX-generated from src/ (Core, HLSL, GLSL, Compiler)
Contributing         → Contributing Guide · Test Shader Corpus · Validation & Evidence Ladder
```

### Reuse map (existing → site)

- `docs/references/compilation-pipeline.md` → Architecture / The Faithful Pipeline
- `docs/glsl-uniform-naming.md` → Architecture / GLSL Dialect Rewrite *(already "as-built")*
- `docs/HOWTO-WASM-KNI.md` → Guides / In-Browser (KNI/Blazor WASM)
- `docs/test-shader-corpus.md` → Contributing / Test Shader Corpus
- `samples/ShaderFiddle.Web/README.md` → Samples / ShaderFiddle.Web
- `README.md` fragments → Home + Getting Started / Overview
- `CLAUDE.md` (distilled) → Contributing / Guide + Validation & Evidence Ladder

### New pages to author

Getting Started: `installation.md`, `in-memory-quickstart.md`, `overview.md`, `restore-native-tools.md` ·
Guides: `mgcb-content-pipeline.md`, `parameters-and-caveats.md` ·
Architecture: `reflection.md`, `mgfx-format.md`, `directx-dxbc-vkd3d.md`, `wasm-frontend.md`, `fx9-preparser-preprocessor.md` ·
Backends: `metal.md`, `vulkan.md`, `directx-in-wasm.md` ·
Samples: `shaderviewer.md`, `mgcb.md` (neither sample has a README yet) ·
CLI: `cli/index.md` · Contributing: `contributing/index.md`, `validation.md` · `api/index.md`.

---

## Content-accuracy guards (do not regress)

The 2026-06-02 sweep fixed these; the site must keep them right:
1. **DirectX = `vkd3d-shader → DXBC`, not `DXC → DXBC`.** DXC emits only SM6 DXIL; DX11 loads DXBC (SM ≤ 5). Pipeline diagrams must show the `vkd3d` leg and the `IDxbcShaderCompiler`/`DxbcBackend` selector.
2. **Metal & MgcbPlugin are stubs** — label "future"/"not implemented"; do not list as shipping backends/packages.
3. **"Same `.mgfx` as `mgfxc`"** means behaviorally equivalent + `Effect`-loadable; **byte-identity is only ShadowDusk's own reproducibility**, never byte-equality with `mgfxc`.
4. **Slang is sample-only / dead reference**; the product in-browser frontend is the **faithful pinned DXC compiled to WASM**.
5. **CLI output is positional** (`mgfxc <SourceFile> <OutputFile>`), no `/Output:`. Note the CLI default `/Profile` is `DirectX_11` while the library `CompilerOptions.Target` default is `OpenGL` — call this out on both the CLI and quickstart pages.

> **Related cleanup (flagged, decide separately):** `CLAUDE.md`'s *Repository Layout* / *tools* / *Tech Stack* sections drifted (omit `ShadowDusk.Compiler`; describe `Metal`/`MgcbPlugin` as working; list `glslang` instead of `vkd3d`; omit `Compiler.Tests`/`ImageTests`/`BrowserTests`). Its pipeline/fidelity/phase-status prose is accurate. Reconciling those structural sections is a small task — track it alongside this phase or as a standalone doc fix.

---

## Tasks

### Doc-comment foundation (the bulk)
- [ ] Enable `GenerateDocumentationFile` per library project (with a `CS1591` transition strategy that never breaks the `TreatWarningsAsErrors` build).
- [ ] Document **ShadowDusk.Core**'s consumer contract first: `IShaderCompiler`(+`CompileAsync`), `CompiledShader`, `CompilerOptions`, `Result<T,TError>`/`Unit`, `ShaderError`(+kind/severity), `PlatformTarget`, then `Reflection/*` and `Preprocessor/*`.
- [ ] Document `EffectCompiler` (class, ctor, `CompileAsync`) and `WasmShaderCompiler.CompileAsync`.
- [ ] Add member-level `<summary>`/`<param>`/`<returns>` to `ISpirvToGlslTranspiler.Transpile`, `SpirvCrossGlslTranspiler`, and the key HLSL backend classes.

### DocFX + site
- [ ] Add `docfx.json` (metadata over the 4 library `.csproj`; build over conceptual content; modern template; search).
- [ ] Write `index.md` (landing, README-synced), `toc.yml`, `api/index.md`, and a `filterConfig.yml` (exclude internals/stubs as needed).
- [ ] Author the new conceptual/how-to/sample/CLI pages; move/include the existing accurate docs (single source of truth).
- [ ] `.gitignore` the generated `api/` and `_site/`.
- [ ] `docfx docfx.json` builds locally with **no empty-summary** API pages for documented types.

### Publish
- [ ] Add `.github/workflows/docs.yml`: checkout (full history) → setup .NET 8 → `dotnet restore --locked-mode` → best-effort `tools/restore.*` (non-fatal) → install pinned DocFX → `docfx docfx.json` → inject base href → `upload-pages-artifact` → `deploy-pages` (permissions: `pages: write`, `id-token: write`; `concurrency: pages`).
- [ ] One-time manual: repo **Settings → Pages → Source = GitHub Actions**.
- [ ] Verify the published site at `…github.io/ShadowDusk/` (nav, search, API pages, inter-page links, base-href under the subpath).

---

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `docfx docfx.json` builds cleanly from a fresh clone (after restore) and emits an API reference for `Core`/`HLSL`/`GLSL`/`Compiler` with **non-empty** summaries for the documented public types — the consumer contract (`IShaderCompiler`, `CompilerOptions`, `CompiledShader`, `Result`, `ShaderError`, `EffectCompiler`) renders with real docs.
- [ ] The site has working Home, Getting Started, Guides, Architecture, Samples, CLI Reference, API Reference, and Contributing sections, with the existing docs reused (not duplicated/divergent).
- [ ] The GitHub Actions workflow builds and deploys to GitHub Pages on push to `main`; the live site loads under the `/ShadowDusk/` subpath with assets/search/links resolving.
- [ ] Stubs (`Metal`, `MgcbPlugin`) are excluded from the API ref and labelled "future"; no page implies they ship.
- [ ] The five *Content-accuracy guards* hold across the site.

## Definition of Done

A public, searchable documentation site at `https://shadowdusk.github.io/ShadowDusk/`,
auto-deployed from `main`, that a newcomer can use to (a) understand what ShadowDusk is
and install it, (b) compile a shader in-memory from a copy-paste C# quickstart, (c) read
how-to guides for the drop-in-`mgfxc`, MGCB, and in-browser paths, (d) understand the
architecture, and (e) browse an accurate API reference generated from the code's own
doc-comments. The site reuses the in-repo docs as one source of truth and never contradicts
the *Content-accuracy guards*.

---

## Open questions / risks

- **Doc-comment volume is the real cost**, concentrated in `ShadowDusk.Core` (~80% of its public surface undocumented). Sequence by consumer visibility; the API ref is only as good as the comments.
- **`TreatWarningsAsErrors` + CS1591**: enabling XML docs naively breaks the build; needs the per-project / `NoWarn` transition above.
- **`ShadowDusk.Wasm` API metadata**: deliberately conceptual-only for now. If type-level API docs are later wanted, add a doc-only `net8.0` extraction TFM — do **not** feed `net8.0-browser` to DocFX metadata.
- **Project-Pages base-href**: the subpath rewrite must cover every emitted HTML; verify search and deep links, not just the landing page. (An org/user `shadowdusk.github.io` repo would serve at root and avoid this, but this is a project repo.)
- **First-run Pages setting** (Source = GitHub Actions) is a manual repo setting the workflow can't perform — document it.
- **Repo URL/owner**: the design assumes `github.com/shadowdusk/ShadowDusk` and `shadowdusk.github.io`. Confirm the actual owner/org (the live remote is `github.com/kaltinril/ShadowDusk`) and set `baseUrl`/`_gitContribute`/base-href accordingly before first publish.
